claim_catalog.statement: Liberty, in its objective form, is nothing other than individual property rights.
Property begins with the human body—with life, will, time, labor, thought, reputation, family, contracts, achievements, land, livelihood, choice, and responsibility.
A human being who does not own his own body is not free.
True religion—if purified of mysticism, state-worship, superstition, anti-rational philosophies, and power-centered interpretations—is the most precise system for the defense of liberty.
Religion came to liberate the human being from servitude to anything other than God.
Islam means submission to God; and submission to God, at the social level, means submission to the rights that God has established for His servants.
Salaam is not merely a verbal greeting; it is a declaration of proprietary peace.
The fundamental human conflict is not between religion and irreligion; it is between religion and mysticism.

Mysticism is any intellectual or political system that separates the human being from property, body, land, reason, revelation, the market, contracts, and liberty, and delivers him to an ambiguous whole.
Iran is a civilizational continuum—the land of proprietors and free people.
The age of artificial intelligence is the age of testing consistency: machines do not tolerate contradiction.
If true religion is a consistent formal system for defending liberty, it must be translatable into the language of machines.

If Liberty is understood objectively, it leads to individual property rights; if individual property rights are pursued to their ultimate foundation, they lead to religion; and if religion is separated from mysticism, it becomes the only consistent formal system capable of defending Liberty.
The acceptance of human free will is a prerequisite for the acceptance of science.
Free will is the only axiom that everyone, even when disagreeing with one another, is inevitably forced to presuppose.
Tawhid (monotheism) is the principle that guarantees liberty by eliminating servitude to anything other than God.
Eliminating Tawhid proliferates axioms and leads to a slide into mysticism and totalitarianism.
The number of axioms and postulates in a formal system must be finite and minimal for it to be valid.
Religion already contains an axiomatic chain (the 'string of beads'); the author did not add to religion but discovered its formal structure.
Welfare is a temporary substitute for religion, and the welfare state onset creates a resource crisis.
Liberty is unstable in a godless world because without resurrection, the last-round problem collapses all deterrence.
Liberty and property rights are prior to any scientific conclusion; they are normative axioms, not scientific findings.
Prophethood is necessary as a barrier against fraud, false messiahs, and new religions.
The finality of prophethood closes the door to the illusion of personal messengership.
The large state is a reflection of social sins.
Good is from God, evil is from man.
Gödel's incompleteness theorems establish that humans are not machines, because humans can perceive truths that no consistent formal system can prove.
A consistent formal system is necessary for understanding true religion.
The formal system of liberty is equivalent to religion.
Common sense is the criterion for detecting religious distortion.
Religion is not merely a reaction to fear; it is a covenant defending liberty.
Iran's civilizational history shows historical independence from Hobbes' Leviathan model.
Iran, religion, and liberty are the three sides of a theory.
The central bank is the 'earthly god' and its monopoly on money creation contradicts liberty.
Inflation causes moral decline through a dopamine-analogous mechanism.
The Suffolk Bank demonstrates the historical viability of free banking and private clearing.
Mudarabah is the natural contractual form under private money and free banking.
Deterministic government is incompatible with volitional liberty.
The denial of free will turns into its own proof (because the act of denying free will presupposes it).
The Austrian School, libertarianism, and classical liberalism are incomplete systems without theological grounding.
Law is discovered, not merely enacted.
An institution is categorically distinct from an organization, and conflating them destroys liberty.
Democracy does not legitimize the choice of the devil.
Democracy is the gateway for collectivist infiltration.
Disruption in sex and family paves the way for the state.
Envy is the root of socialism.
Taxation is a penalty for creativity.
State education reproduces slavery.
The story of Adam and Eve is a question of property rights.
Hobbes is more honest than Locke and Rothbard, yet he is still inconsistent.
Without Mahdism, a theory of liberty cannot become fully consistent.
Property ownership lowers time preference and thereby builds civilization.
Iranian myths (Shahnameh) are the civilizational memory of liberty.
Zahhak and Iblis represent the universal pattern of state, power, and deception.
Religion is the antithesis of the state.
Ghadir Khumm transferred wilayah (guardianship), not a political state.
Islam entered Iran not only by the sword but as a theory of transformation compatible with Iranian civilization.
History cannot be understood without theory.
Iran's crisis has two roots: the distortion of religion and the theft of history.
The Iranian nation is defined by liberty and property rights.
Jizya was economically more favorable to Iranians than Sassanid taxation, explaining Islam's appeal.
Statism is Shirk (theological term for polytheism or associating partners with God).
Liberty is not primarily an economic issue; before anything else it is a theological question.
God has established two proofs for humanity: one outward (prophets and Imams) and one inward (reason).
Rothbard has four theoretical gaps that prevent his libertarianism from being complete.
Natural law and natural rights, grounded in subject-centered reason without revelation, cannot withstand the state or the majority.
The rule of Taslīṭ is the religious form of individual property rights and the criterion for evaluating narrations.
The problem of the West is not liberty; it is intervention.
Usury is the most compressed expression of the entire problem of state-controlled money.
If Iran, in reaction to a distorted governmentalized religion, reproduces Europe's post-Church trajectory (mysticism → Hegel → socialism), the result will be a sea of blood.
The theory of liberty is the only existing solution for the ethical control of artificial intelligence.
A civilization without consistent principles collapses under its own contradictions.
Gödel believed mathematical objects are real entities we perceive (Platonism/Objectivism in mathematics).
Hegel, Marx, fascism, and communism are modern recyclings of the same ancient structure of mysticism and domination.
Every intervention in the market is a step toward human servitude; modern states are new idol-temples.
There is no consistent historical record of racist ideologies or racial massacres in Iran.
The coup of 19 August 1953 represents the distortion/theft of Iranian history to control the present and future of Iranians.
If Shi'ism were truly consistent with socialism and fascism, Iran would already be finished.
Bahram Gur established a charter of limited kingship calling people kings over their own possessions.
Dense urban concentration creates exponential commons problems and erodes liberty.
